SUMMARY: NESTED | FIELD | CONSTR | METHOD DETAIL: FIELD | CONSTR | METHOD

com.datajets
Class DataJet

java.lang.Object
  com.datajets.DataJet
All Implemented Interfaces:
java.lang.Cloneable
Direct Known Subclasses:
GenericDataJet
public abstract class DataJet
extends java.lang.Object
implements java.lang.Cloneable

The base class for all DataJets. DataJet provies protected methods for all basic database operations (create, read, update, and delete), but it exposes very little of that functionality publicly. To create a useful class, write new methods that expose the functionality.

To use DataJet's methods, the class must first be initialized with connection, table and field information. The connection information can be supplied as driver and connection string, or as an object that implements ConnectionGenerator. Table and fields must both be defined. If any of this initialization information is missing or incorrect, DataJet will throw exceptions when calling its various methods -- ConnectionException if the connection information is incorrect and DataJetException if the table or field information is incorrect. The following listing shows how to create a basic class that extends DataJet and provides methods for loading all records in the database: 

import com.datajets.*;

class MyDataJet extends DataJet {
        public static JetSet jetSet() {
                return new JetSet( [insert database driver],
                        [insert connection url], 
                        "my_table",
                        new String[]{"id", "field1", "field2"}
                );
        }

        public MyDataJet() {
                super(jetSet());
        }

        public String getField1() {
                return getFieldValue(1).toString();
        }

        public int getField2() {
                return getFieldValue(2).toInt();
        }

        public static MyDataJet[] loadAll() throws DataJetException {
                MyDataJet[] dj = new MyDataJet[]{new MyDataJet()};
                JetSet jset = jetSet();
                SqlBuilder sql = new SqlBuilder(jset.getTable(), jset.getFieldList());
                return (MyDataJet[])MyDataJet.loadMultiple(sql, dj, jset);
        }
}
The above DataJet will be able to read all records from the database. For example: 
//Example: read all records from the database
MyDataJet[] jets = MyDataJet.loadAll();
for ( int i = 0; i < jets.length; i++ ) {
        System.out.println( jets[i].getField1() + " " + jets[i].getField2() );
}
To learn more about DataJet, please see the tutorials on DataJets.com.

Author:
David M. Anderson
See Also:
GenericDataJet, Storable, Removable

          Loads multiple records from the database to the supplied array of DataJet objects.

          Removes the current DataJet from the database.

equals, finalize, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait
 

Constructor Detail

DataJet

protected DataJet(int id,
                  JetSet jset)
Constructor that sets the id for the DataJet to use. Please note that this creation method does not load the record into the DataJet from the database. Use the static load method for this.

Parameters:
id - integer representing the identity field value
jset - JetSet containing properties for the DataJet
See Also:
load(int, com.datajets.DataJet, com.datajets.JetSet)

DataJet

protected DataJet(JetSet jset)
Constructor.

Parameters:
jset - JetSet containing properties for the DataJet
Method Detail

getId

public int getId()
Returns the value of the identity field for the currently-loaded DataJet. If no record is loaded, getId returns zero (0);

Returns:
int value of identity field

recordExists

protected boolean recordExists()
Tests whether the currently-loaded DataJet has a corresponding record in the database.

Returns:
true if there is a corresponding record; false otherwise.

setFieldValue

protected void setFieldValue(int fieldIndex,
                             int newvalue)
Sets the value of a field.

Parameters:
fieldIndex - zero-based ordinal position of field in fieldList (defined when initializing the DataJet)
newvalue - int value to set the field to

setFieldValue

                             boolean newvalue)

Parameters:
fieldIndex - zero-based ordinal position of field in fieldList (defined when initializing the DataJet)
newvalue - boolean value to set the field to

loadSql

protected static java.lang.String loadSql(int idToLoad,
                                          JetSet jset)
Returns SQL statement the DataJet will use to load a record.

Parameters:
idToLoad - int value representing the identity of the record to load
jset - JetSet containing properties for the DataJet
Returns:
string SQL statement

fillDataJet

protected static void fillDataJet(DataJet dataJetToFill,
                                  java.lang.Object[] fieldValues)
Sets the field values of the supplied DataJet.

Parameters:
dataJetToFill - instance of a DataJet to set values of
fieldValues - values to set fields to

load

protected static DataJet load(int idToLoad,
                              DataJet dataJetToFill,
                              JetSet jset)
                       throws DataJetException
Loads a single record from the database to the supplied DataJet.

Parameters:
idToLoad - value of identity field for record to load
dataJetToFill - instance of DataJet to use fill with values from the database
jset - JetSet containing properties for the DataJet
Returns:
DataJet representing the record loaded from the database
Throws:
DataJetException - if the DataJet cannot be loaded or if the connection information is incorrect

loadMultiple

protected static DataJet[] loadMultiple(java.lang.String sql,
                                        DataJet[] dataJetsToFill,
                                        JetSet jset)
                                 throws DataJetException
Loads multiple records from the database to the supplied array of DataJet objects.

Parameters:
sql - SQL statement to use to return the records from the database
dataJetsToFill - array of DataJets to fill with values from the database
jset - JetSet containing properties for the DataJet
Returns:
array of DataJet objects representing the records loaded from the database
Throws:
DataJetException - if the DataJets cannot be loaded or if the connection information is incorrect

loadMultiple

protected static DataJet[] loadMultiple(SqlBuilder sql,
                                        DataJet[] dataJetsToFill,
                                        JetSet jset)
                                 throws DataJetException
Loads multiple records from the database to the supplied array of DataJet objects.

Parameters:
sql - SqulBuilder to use to return the records from the database
dataJetsToFill - array of DataJets to fill with values from the database
jset - JetSet containing properties for the DataJet
Returns:
array of DataJet objects representing the records loaded from the database
Throws:
DataJetException - if the DataJet has not been initialized or if the connection information is incorrect
See Also:
SqlBuilder

loadMultiple

protected static java.util.ArrayList loadMultiple(SqlBuilder sql,
                                                  DataJet dataJetToFill,
                                                  JetSet jset)
                                           throws DataJetException
Loads multiple records from the database to the supplied array of DataJet objects.

Parameters:
sql - SqulBuilder to use to return the records from the database
dataJetToFill - DataJet to fill with values from the database
jset - JetSet containing properties for the DataJet
Returns:
ArrayList containing DataJet objects representing the records loaded from the database
Throws:
DataJetException - if the DataJet has not been initialized or if the connection information is incorrect
See Also:
SqlBuilder

loadMultiple

protected static java.util.ArrayList loadMultiple(java.lang.String sql,
                                                  DataJet dataJetToFill,
                                                  JetSet jset)

Parameters:
sql - SQL statement to use to return the records from the database

jset - JetSet containing properties for the DataJet

Throws:
DataJetException - if the DataJet has not been initialized or if the connection information is incorrect

store

protected void store(java.sql.Connection con)
              throws DataJetException
Stores the current DataJet in the database. This method can be used to add a new DataJet or to update an existing one.

Parameters:
con - java.sql.Connection object to use for the operation
Throws:
DataJetException - if the DataJet has not been initialized or if the connection to the database can not be established

store

protected void store()
              throws DataJetException
Stores the current DataJet in the database. This method can be used to add a new DataJet or to update an existing one.

Throws:
DataJetException - if the DataJet has not been initialized or if the connection to the database can not be established

storeSql

protected java.lang.String storeSql()
Returns SQL statement the DataJet will use to store a record.

Returns:
string SQL statement
Throws:
DataJetException - if the table or field list have not been specified
